svg.text: Fast & Slow Pointers — Floyd's Tortoise and Hare
Slow advances 1 step, fast advances 2 steps. If a cycle exists, fast laps slow inside it.
If fast reaches null (or end), no cycle. O(n) time, O(1) space.
Once both are inside the cycle of length C, the gap between them shrinks by 1 per step.
After at most C steps the gap reaches 0 — they collide.
After meeting, reset slow to head. Walk both at speed 1 — they meet at the cycle start.
• Middle of linked list (876): when fast hits end, slow is at the middle.
• Palindrome linked list (234): find middle, reverse second half, compare.
• Reorder list (143): same — find middle, reverse, interleave.
• Happy number (202): treat digit-square sum as a function f; detect cycle in f^k(n).
• Find duplicate number (287): treat array as f(i) = A[i]; the duplicate is the cycle entry.
Anywhere a sequence x, f(x), f²(x), ... eventually cycles, Floyd applies.
